questions.question: A construction company needs to package $48$ boxes of nails into crates. If each crate can hold $6$ boxes, how many crates are needed?
A recipe for making cookies calls for $315$ grams of flour. If you want to make $ rac{1}{3}$ of the recipe, how many grams of flour will you need?
A builder is constructing a house with $270$ bricks. If the bricks are stacked in rows of $9$ bricks, how many rows can be made?
A chef needs to package $96$ cupcakes into boxes of $8$. How many boxes are needed?
A bridge is $1440$ meters long. If it is divided into sections of $16$ meters, how many sections are there?
A group of friends want to share some candy equally. If they have $48$ pieces of candy and there are $8$ friends, how many pieces will each friend get?
A bookshelf has $5$ shelves, and each shelf can hold $9$ books. If the bookshelf is currently empty, how many books can be placed on it in total?
A water tank can hold $1800$ liters of water. If $360$ liters of water are already in the tank, what fraction of the tank is filled?

A carpenter is building a staircase with $18$ steps. If each step requires $4$ wooden planks, and the carpenter has $72$ planks available, will they have enough planks to complete the staircase? Show your work and explain your answer.

A bakery is making a large cake that requires $5$ kilograms of flour. If they have a $25$-kilogram bag of flour, and they want to make $ rac{3}{5}$ of the cake recipe, how much flour will they use? Calculate the amount of flour needed and show your work.

teacher_rules: Use the divisibility rules to check your answers
Show all work for free-response questions
Check your units and make sure they are correct
